activities_projects.title: Hydrological Cycle Model
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate the water cycle and its processes
steps: Fill the container with water to represent a water body
Cover with plastic wrap and place in sunlight or under a lamp
Observe evaporation, condensation, and precipitation within the model
Label and explain each process in a notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the water cycle and its components
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation accuracy, explanation clarity, and model demonstration (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Groundwater Recharge Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Study how soil type affects groundwater recharge
steps: Fill jars with different soil types (sand, clay, gravel)
Pour equal amounts of water into each jar
Observe and record how fast water passes through each soil type
Analyze which soil allows better recharge
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about infiltration and groundwater recharge
activities_projects.evaluation: Experiment setup, observation accuracy, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of River Management Projects
activities_projects.objective: Analyze large-scale water management initiatives in India
steps: Select two major river projects (e.g., Bhakra Nangal Dam and Mahanadi Delta projects)
Research objectives, benefits, and environmental/social impact
Write a comparative report highlighting advantages and challenges
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand human intervention in river management and its consequences
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research, clarity, and critical analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water Quality Analysis Lab
activities_projects.objective: Test and compare chemical and biological indicators in water samples
materials: Water samples from river, pond, and tap
Test strips for hardness, nitrates, or chlorine
steps: Collect water samples from different sources
Test pH, turbidity, and other parameters
Compare results and classify water as safe or unsafe
Discuss potential sources of contamination
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop hands-on knowledge of water testing and pollution analysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, observations, and conclusion clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rainwater Harvesting Design and Cost Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Plan and analyze a feasible rainwater harvesting system
steps: Design a rainwater harvesting system for your school or home
Include collection, filtration, and storage components
Estimate construction cost and annual water savings
Prepare a report with diagrams and calculations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect practical design with economic and environmental benefits
activities_projects.evaluation: Design feasibility, calculation accuracy, and report clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Field Survey: Water Usage and Conservation Practices
activities_projects.objective: Investigate real-life water practices in your community
steps: Prepare a survey on water sources, consumption, and conservation measures in 10 households
Collect data on daily usage, water-saving practices, and challenges faced
Analyze results and present findings in charts or tables
Propose realistic solutions based on observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop research, analysis, and presentation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Survey accuracy, data interpretation, and quality of recommendations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Impact of Water Pollution on Aquatic Life Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how polluted water affects biodiversity
steps: Research a water body affected by pollution (local or national level)
Identify aquatic species affected and type of pollutants involved
Prepare a detailed report with causes, effects, and possible mitigation measures
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand ecological consequences of water pollution
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research, clarity, and logical recommendations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Design an Awareness Campaign on Water Conservation
activities_projects.objective: Promote water-saving behavior in the community
steps: Create a multi-pronged campaign including posters, slogans, and social media messages
Highlight local water issues and conservation tips
Present your campaign to the class or community
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop advocacy and communication skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Effectiveness, creativity, and clarity of message (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study: Traditional vs Modern Irrigation Techniques
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate effectiveness and sustainability of irrigation methods
steps: Research traditional methods (wells, tanks, canals) and modern methods (drip, sprinkler, tube wells)
Compare efficiency, water use, and environmental impact
Prepare a detailed table or report with examples from India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand technological evolution and sustainability considerations in water management
activities_projects.evaluation: Research quality, analysis depth, and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Local Water Body Conservation Project
activities_projects.objective: Plan a practical project to conserve or restore a water body
steps: Identify a nearby pond, lake, or river in need of conservation
Assess current condition, pollution sources, and human usage
Prepare a project plan including clean-up, pollution control, and awareness measures
Optionally, implement small-scale actions with community support
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain hands-on experience in environmental conservation and project planning
activities_projects.evaluation: Project feasibility, planning quality, and reflection (10 marks)
